context.projectOverview: Fleet Expand — Phase 2 of the fleet orchestrator. A `fleet expand <goal>` subcommand turns one high-level goal into a Zod-valid FleetManifest via a single DeepSeek LLMClient call plus one bounded coercion re-prompt, then writes-and-stops. This sprint (1 of 2) builds ONLY the pure src/fleet/decomposer.ts module + collocated tests — no CLI, no spawn, no real network. Additive on top of the already-merged Phase 1 fleet runner.
relevantFiles: src/providers/types.ts (LLMClient, ChatParams: jsonObjectMode vs responseSchema, ChatResponse.text)
src/fleet/manifest.ts (FleetManifestSchema, FleetChildSchema — reuse verbatim; config optional so extra guard needed)

principles: See .bober/principles.md — ESM everywhere with .js import extensions (NodeNext); provider-agnostic interfaces (never import an SDK outside providers/); Zod for validation; contract-based JSON state in .bober/; async fs only (node:fs/promises, no *Sync); use `import type` (consistent-type-imports); no explicit any (use unknown + narrowing); prefix unused params with _; unicode box-drawing section comments `// -- Name ------`; conventional commits, sprint commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
notes: Read the Sprint Briefing FIRST: .bober/briefings/sprint-spec-20260617-fleet-expand-decomposer-1-briefing.md. It contains real code snippets for all reuse targets. CRITICAL gotchas: (1) use jsonObjectMode:true NOT responseSchema (DeepSeek rejects strict json_schema); (2) the per-child config-key guard must run IN ADDITION to FleetManifestSchema.safeParse (FleetChildSchema.config is optional so safeParse alone accepts it); (3) decomposeGoal must NEVER resolve with an invalid/partial manifest — throw with formatted Zod issues on exhaustion; (4) at most 2 client.chat calls total (DECOMPOSE_MAX_RETRIES=1); (5) tests use a fake LLMClient only — no real network, no fs.
